name: East-Asian curved roof
description: An East-Asian roof with curved upturned (flying) eaves: a deep eave shelf, an inward-sloping fascia lip, and horns that sweep up at each corner capped with a gilt finial. Variants cover a single hip-and-upturn, a multi-tier pagoda, a temple double-eave, an irimoya hip-and-gable, and a gable with upturned ends. Reads as a Chinese palace roof in red-and-yellow or a charcoal Japanese roof depending on style/palette.
placement_rules: Sits on the top storey's footprint, rising from the wall top.
The eave shelf overhangs the walls by 1-3 cells; corner horns sweep up by `flare` blocks.
Pagoda variant stacks `tier_count` shrinking eave tiers, each upper tier centred over the one below (upper footprint always within the lower).
Best on square or near-square footprints so the four corner horns are symmetric.
lighting_ventilation: Roof element only; the deep eaves shade the upper windows and shed rain clear of the walls.

chinese.notes: Imperial palace: vermilion drum walls, imperial-yellow roof tiles, gilt finials.

japanese.notes: Zen restraint: charcoal tiles, dark timber drum, muted finials.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.asian_roof.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
